status_notes: Initial research from AWS Config documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live AWS environment).
notes: Configuration recording, change tracking, and compliance evaluation. Records all resource configurations and changes over time.

data.description: Records the configuration of AWS resources in the account. Captures the current state and tracks changes over time. Can record all supported resource types (recommended) or specific resource types only. Creates configuration items for each resource state change. Configuration history is stored for the retention period (default 7 years).

options.description: Record all supported resource types. Recommended for comprehensive visibility. Includes future resource types automatically.
Record only selected resource types. Reduces cost but may miss security-relevant resources.
Record global resources (IAM users, groups, roles, policies). Enable in one region only to avoid duplicates.

default.note: Configuration recorder must be explicitly created and started.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable the configuration recorder for all resource types in all regions. Record global resources in one region only. Ensure the recorder is started and not paused.

data.description: Rules that evaluate whether AWS resources comply with desired configurations. Three types: AWS managed rules (300+ pre-built rules maintained by AWS), custom rules (Lambda-based, your logic), and CloudFormation Guard rules (declarative, policy-as-code). Rules are evaluated on configuration changes, on a schedule, or both.

options.description: 300+ pre-built rules. Examples: s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited, iam-password-policy, encrypted-volumes, multi-region-cloudtrail-enabled.
Your own compliance logic implemented as Lambda functions. Full flexibility.
Declarative rules using CloudFormation Guard policy-as-code language. No Lambda required.

data.vendor_recommendation: Start with AWS managed rules for common compliance checks. Add custom rules for organization-specific requirements. Use Guard rules for declarative policy-as-code.

match_nodes.description: Groups of Config rules deployed together for compliance frameworks

data.description: Pre-packaged collections of Config rules and remediation actions that can be deployed as a single unit. AWS provides sample conformance packs aligned with compliance frameworks. Can be deployed to individual accounts or across an organization. Custom conformance packs can be authored in YAML.

options.description: Rules aligned with CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark controls
Rules aligned with NIST SP 800-53 security controls
Rules aligned with PCI Data Security Standard requirements
Service-specific best practice rules (S3, IAM, EC2, etc.)
Author your own conformance pack in YAML with rules and remediation

data.vendor_recommendation: Deploy conformance packs aligned with your compliance requirements across the organization. Use organization conformance packs for centralized deployment and compliance tracking.

data.description: Aggregates Config data from multiple accounts and regions into a single view. Provides centralized compliance dashboard across an AWS Organization. Supports source accounts (individual accounts) or organization-wide aggregation. Read-only — does not modify source data.

default.note: Requires explicit creation and authorization from source accounts (or organization access).

data.vendor_recommendation: Create an organization-wide aggregator in a centralized security account. Use it for cross-account compliance visibility and reporting.

match_nodes.description: Automatic remediation via SSM Automation documents

data.description: Automatic or manual remediation of non-compliant resources using AWS Systems Manager (SSM) Automation documents. Remediation actions are associated with Config rules. When a rule evaluates a resource as non-compliant, the remediation action can automatically bring it into compliance. Supports auto-remediation (triggered on evaluation) or manual remediation (on-demand).

options.description: Automatically execute remediation when non-compliance is detected. Configurable retry attempts and backoff.
On-demand remediation triggered by an administrator from the console or CLI.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use auto-remediation for well-understood compliance rules with safe, idempotent remediation actions. Test remediation actions in non-production environments first. Use manual remediation for rules where automatic action could cause disruption.

audit.console: AWS Console → Config → Rules → {rule} → Remediation action

match_nodes.description: Where Config sends configuration snapshots and change notifications

data.description: Defines where AWS Config delivers configuration snapshots and configuration change notifications. Configuration snapshots are delivered to an S3 bucket. Change notifications can be sent to an SNS topic. Only one delivery channel per account per region.

options.description: Configuration snapshots and configuration history files. Required.
Configuration change notifications and compliance change notifications. Optional.

default.value: Must be configured when setting up Config
default.note: An S3 bucket is required. SNS topic is optional but recommended for real-time notification.

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure an S3 bucket in a centralized logging account. Enable SNS notifications for real-time alerting on configuration changes. Use S3 lifecycle policies for cost management.

audit.console: AWS Console → Config → Settings → Delivery method
